name: St. Louis Drive Inn
tagline: Old-guard plate lunch drive-inn near Chaminade — formica tables, walk-up counter, and food that tastes like someone's tutu made it.
mustOrder: fried chicken plate — crispy and generous
parking: Small lot on Waialae Ave; street parking available
talkStory: One of the old-guard drive-inns that defined the plate lunch genre in Honolulu. Decades old, no-frills, two scoops rice, mac salad, protein — the formula that built Hawaii's lunch culture. The crowd is Chaminade students, Kaimuki workers, and families who've been coming for generations. Rainbow Drive-In gets the fame; St. Louis Drive Inn is the quieter neighborhood equivalent. Opens early, closes mid-afternoon.
